theme: match both the roughness and the gaps
title: Digit-Selection Carpet: Matching Roughness and Gaps
strategies: single-cardinality floor baseline: round n^D* DOWN to an integer N, keep the first N cells of the n x n grid in row-major order at every level (matches neither target well)
single-cardinality rounded greedy: round n^D* to the NEAREST integer N, keep the first N row-major cells at every level (nails the dimension, still ignores lacunarity entirely -- the trap)

decoupled search: search a small set of per-level cardinality schedules to fit D* (allowing two different cardinalities across levels to hit a fractional target), THEN independently search cell-placement patterns per level (compact/blob vs maximally-dispersed farthest-point vs mixed) to fit Lam*, since the dimension of a digit-selection carpet depends only on the per-level cardinalities while lacunarity depends on which cells are chosen
